notes: Gold consumable rules for two tables: program_career_paths (unitid x cipcode x soc_code, ~626K rows) and career_branches (soc_code x related_soc_code, 15,944 rows). Row count range widened from spec estimate (150K-500K) to EDA-observed 580K-700K. stat_res and boss_ai_score are confirmed 100% null placeholders. Golden dataset rule DEFERRED pending golden dataset creation by @primary-agent. Concept normalization patterns (CONS-COLLISION-RESOLVED, CONS-COVERAGE-FLOOR) do not apply -- this is a cross-source join spec, not a concept normalization spec. No concept normalization maps are in use.

rules.name: Grain uniqueness: unitid x cipcode x soc_code
rules.sql: SELECT unitid, cipcode, soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.program_career_paths GROUP BY unitid, cipcode, soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per unitid x cipcode x soc_code. Any duplicate indicates dedup failure after CIP prefix fan-out. Maps to patterns CONS-GRAIN-UNIQUE and ADV-GRAIN-UNIQUE.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'After dedup on (unitid, cipcode, soc_code), this collapses to 626,406 rows.' 47.8% of grain tuples had duplicates before dedup (up to 37 per grain). This rule verifies the dedup succeeded. Logical model: 'Zero duplicates on (unitid, cipcode, soc_code). Enforced at promote time after dedup.' Threshold: 100% unique.

rules.name: Record ID uniqueness and not null (program_career_paths)
rules.sql: SELECT record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.program_career_paths GROUP BY record_id HAVING cnt > 1 UNION ALL SELECT 'NULL_CHECK' AS record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE record_id IS NULL HAVING cnt > 0
rules.description: Surrogate key record_id (computed via compute_grain_id with prefix 'pcp') must be non-null and unique. Duplicate or null record_id indicates a grain hash collision or derivation bug.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'record_id is PK, NOT NULL.' Deterministic SHA-256 hash of natural key (unitid, cipcode, soc_code) should produce 0 collisions for ~626K rows. Established pattern from GLD-CO-002 and GLD-OP-002.

rules.name: Row count within expected range (program_career_paths)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) >= 580000 AND COUNT(*) <= 700000 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.program_career_paths
rules.description: Gold table row count must be within 580,000-700,000. EDA-observed count is 626,406. This range accommodates normal variation from CIP prefix fan-out and crosswalk updates.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Row count estimate is 626,406 -- EXCEEDS spec range.' EDA recommendation: 'Widen DQ row count range to at least 600,000-700,000.' Widened further to 580,000 lower bound to accommodate potential crosswalk coverage changes. The spec estimate of 150K-500K was based on pre-EDA projections; the actual CIP prefix fan-out produces median 5 SOC codes per CIP4 prefix with heavy-tail CIPs like 52.02 mapping to 43 SOC codes.

rules.name: CIP prefix join coverage >= 90% of distinct CIPs
rules.sql: WITH scorecard_cips AS (SELECT DISTINCT cipcode FROM consumable.career_outcomes), matched_cips AS (SELECT DISTINCT cipcode FROM consumable.program_career_paths) SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM matched_cips) * 1.0 / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM scorecard_cips), 0) >= 0.90 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: At least 90% of distinct CIP codes from career_outcomes must appear in program_career_paths, confirming the 4-digit CIP prefix join is working. The ~9% unmatched CIPs are XX.99/XX.00 catch-all categories with no crosswalk mapping.
rules.rationale: EDA report: '355 of 390 Scorecard CIP codes find at least one crosswalk match via 4-digit prefix (91.0%).' The 35 unmatched CIPs are predominantly XX.99 'Other' and XX.00 'General' catch-all categories. Threshold set at 90% to allow minor variation. P1 because known edge cases (catch-all CIPs) account for the gap.

rules.name: CIP prefix row coverage >= 95%
rules.sql: WITH scorecard_rows AS (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.career_outcomes), matched_rows AS (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT unitid || '|' || cipcode) AS cnt FROM consumable.program_career_paths) SELECT CASE WHEN matched_rows.cnt * 1.0 / NULLIF(scorecard_rows.cnt, 0) >= 0.95 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM scorecard_rows, matched_rows
rules.description: At least 95% of career_outcomes rows (by distinct unitid+cipcode) must appear in program_career_paths. The unmatched 2.9% rows are from niche catch-all CIP categories.
rules.rationale: EDA report: '97.1% of Scorecard rows are covered. The ~9% of CIPs without matches tend to be small/niche programs accounting for only 2,008 rows (2.9%).' Threshold set at 95% with margin. P1 because the loss is expected and documented.

rules.name: stat_ern range 1-10 when non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE stat_ern IS NOT NULL AND (stat_ern < 1 OR stat_ern > 10)
rules.description: Earning Power stat must be in the 1-10 integer range when non-null. Values outside this range indicate a derivation bug in the ERN formula. Maps to pattern CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'stat_ern simulated distribution uses full range 1-10, roughly normal centered around 5-6.' The derivation formula ROUND(1 + 9 * raw_ern) with raw_ern in [0,1] mathematically constrains output to [1,10]. Logical model: 'numeric, 1-10 scale.' Any violation is a formula or input corruption bug.

rules.name: stat_roi range 1-10 when non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE stat_roi IS NOT NULL AND (stat_roi < 1 OR stat_roi > 10)
rules.description: Return on Investment stat must be in the 1-10 integer range when non-null. Piecewise linear derivation is bounded by floor=1 and cap=10. Maps to pattern CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'stat_roi simulated distribution: heavily right-skewed, most programs score 8-10, only 2 programs at score 1.' The piecewise derivation has explicit floor (1) and cap (10). Logical model: 'numeric, 1-10 scale.' Any violation is a formula bug.

rules.name: stat_grw range 1-10 when non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE stat_grw IS NOT NULL AND (stat_grw < 1 OR stat_grw > 10)
rules.description: Growth stat must be in the 1-10 integer range when non-null. Carried directly from occupation_profiles.grw_score_rounded, which is validated by GLD-OP-005. Maps to pattern CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'grw_score_rounded range 1-10, 0% null in occupation_profiles.' Upstream DQ rule GLD-OP-005 enforces this range at source. Any violation indicates a join or carry-forward bug.

rules.name: stat_hmn range 1-10 when non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE stat_hmn IS NOT NULL AND (stat_hmn < 1 OR stat_hmn > 10)
rules.description: Human Edge stat must be in the 1-10 integer range when non-null. Carried from onet_work_profiles.hmn_score_rounded. Maps to pattern CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'hmn_score_rounded range 1-10 when present, 3.0% null (24 partial profiles).' Upstream DQ validates this. Any out-of-range value is a carry-forward bug.

rules.name: stat_res is 100% null (placeholder)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE stat_res IS NOT NULL
rules.description: AI Resilience stat is a placeholder -- must be null for every row in MVP. Any non-null value indicates premature population or data corruption.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'stat_res: 0 computable rows (0.0%), Placeholder (always null).' Spec: 'PLACEHOLDER -- requires Karpathy scores + task-level AI scoring (separate spec). Set to null for hackathon MVP.' Logical model: 'PLACEHOLDER: always null in MVP.'

rules.name: boss_ai_score is 100% null (placeholder)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE boss_ai_score IS NOT NULL
rules.description: AI Boss score is a placeholder -- must be null for every row in MVP. Same dependency as stat_res.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'boss_ai: 0 computable rows (0.0%).' Spec: 'PLACEHOLDER -- same dependency as stat_res. Null until Karpathy integration.' Logical model: 'PLACEHOLDER: always null in MVP. Same dependency as stat_res.'

rules.name: boss_loans_score range 1-10 when non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE boss_loans_score IS NOT NULL AND (boss_loans_score < 1 OR boss_loans_score > 10)
rules.description: Student Loans Boss score must be in the 1-10 integer range when non-null. Derived as 11 - stat_roi, so mathematically constrained to [1,10]. Maps to pattern CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE.
rules.rationale: Derivation rule: boss_loans_score = 11 - stat_roi. Since stat_roi is bounded [1,10] (GLD-FE-007), boss_loans is bounded [1,10]. EDA report: 'boss_loans computable for 37.6% of rows.' Any out-of-range value is a derivation bug.

rules.name: boss_market_score range 1-10 when non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE boss_market_score IS NOT NULL AND (boss_market_score < 1 OR boss_market_score > 10)
rules.description: Market Boss score must be in the 1-10 integer range when non-null. Carried from occupation_profiles.market_score_rounded. Maps to pattern CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE.
rules.rationale: Upstream DQ rule validates market_score_rounded range at source. EDA report: 'boss_market computable for 97.4% of rows.' Any out-of-range value is a carry-forward bug.

rules.name: boss_burnout_score range 1-10 when non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE boss_burnout_score IS NOT NULL AND (boss_burnout_score < 1 OR boss_burnout_score > 10)
rules.description: Burnout Boss score must be in the 1-10 integer range when non-null. Carried from onet_work_profiles.burnout_score_rounded. Maps to pattern CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'boss_burnout computable for 93.0% of rows.' Upstream DQ validates burnout_score_rounded. Any out-of-range value is a carry-forward bug.

rules.name: boss_ceiling_score range 1-10 when non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE boss_ceiling_score IS NOT NULL AND (boss_ceiling_score < 1 OR boss_ceiling_score > 10)
rules.description: Ceiling Boss score must be in the 1-10 integer range when non-null. Derived as ROUND(10 - 9 * wage_percentile_education_tier) with input in [0,1], constraining output to [1,10]. Maps to pattern CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE.
rules.rationale: Derivation rule constrains output to [1,10] mathematically when input percentile is in [0,1]. EDA report: 'boss_ceiling_score uniform distribution (by design).' Upstream occupation_profiles wage_percentile_education_tier is validated in [0,1]. Any violation is a derivation or input corruption bug.

rules.name: match_quality valid enum values
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE match_quality IS NULL OR match_quality NOT IN ('full', 'partial_no_onet', 'partial_no_bls', 'scorecard_only')
rules.description: match_quality must be NOT NULL and one of the four defined categories. This field is derived at Gold time from actual join results. Null or unexpected values indicate derivation failure.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'NOT NULL, enum: full/partial_no_onet/partial_no_bls/scorecard_only.' Spec: 'Derived at Gold time from actual join results, NOT from upstream crosswalk Silver has_scorecard_match flag.' EDA report confirms these four values with distribution: 93.2% full, 4.2% partial_no_onet, 2.5% partial_no_bls, 0.1% scorecard_only.

rules.name: match_quality 'full' >= 90% of rows
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN match_quality = 'full' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(*) >= 0.90 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.program_career_paths
rules.description: At least 90% of rows should have 'full' match quality (both BLS and O*NET data present). A significant drop indicates join chain degradation.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'match_quality distribution: 93.2% full.' Threshold set at 90% to allow variation from upstream data changes. The high 'full' rate reflects strong BLS/O*NET SOC coverage (773 of 867 crosswalk SOCs have both sources). P1 because minor variation is expected.

rules.name: overall_confidence valid enum values
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE overall_confidence IS NULL OR overall_confidence NOT IN ('high', 'medium', 'low')
rules.description: overall_confidence must be NOT NULL and one of 'high', 'medium', 'low'. Derived from stats_available_count and match_quality.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'NOT NULL, enum: high/medium/low.' Derivation rule fully specifies the three tiers. EDA report confirms distribution: 33.9% high, 2.6% medium, 63.5% low. Any null or unexpected value is a derivation bug.

rules.name: overall_confidence 'high' >= 25% of rows
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN overall_confidence = 'high' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(*) >= 0.25 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.program_career_paths
rules.description: At least 25% of rows should have 'high' overall confidence. A drop below this indicates stat availability regression.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'overall_confidence: 33.9% high.' The high tier requires 4+ stats AND full match quality. The 33.9% rate is driven by Scorecard earnings/debt availability (~36% non-null). Threshold set at 25% with margin for Scorecard data variation. P1 because the primary driver is upstream Scorecard privacy suppression, not a bug.

rules.name: stats_available_count range 0-4 (MVP)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE stats_available_count IS NULL OR stats_available_count < 0 OR stats_available_count > 4
rules.description: stats_available_count must be NOT NULL and 0-4 in MVP (max 4 because stat_res is always null). Count of 5 would indicate stat_res was incorrectly populated.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'stats_available_count distribution: 0 (0.2%), 1 (5.4%), 2 (53.3%), 3 (7.2%), 4 (33.9%). Maximum 4 in MVP since stat_res is always null.' Logical model: 'NOT NULL, count of non-null pentagon stats (0-5).' Capped at 4 for MVP validation. Any value of 5 contradicts the stat_res placeholder rule.

rules.name: stats_available_count >= 2 for 90%+ of rows
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN stats_available_count >= 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(*) >= 0.90 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.program_career_paths
rules.description: At least 90% of rows should have 2 or more stats available. Most rows get at least GRW + HMN from BLS/O*NET coverage. Rows with 0-1 stats indicate join chain failures.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'stats_available_count >= 2 accounts for 94.4% of rows (53.3% + 7.2% + 33.9%).' Only 5.6% have fewer than 2 stats. Threshold set at 90% with margin. GRW (97.4% computable) and HMN (93.0% computable) provide the floor.

rules.name: bosses_available_count range 0-4 (MVP)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE bosses_available_count IS NULL OR bosses_available_count < 0 OR bosses_available_count > 4
rules.description: bosses_available_count must be NOT NULL and 0-4 in MVP (max 4 because boss_ai_score is always null).
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'bosses_available_count distribution: 0 (0.2%), 1 (1.8%), 2 (5.4%), 3 (58.7%), 4 (33.9%). Maximum 4 in MVP since boss_ai_score is always null.' Logical model: 'NOT NULL, count of non-null boss fight scores (0-5).' Capped at 4 for MVP.

rules.name: boss_loans_score = 11 - stat_roi (consistency check)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE stat_roi IS NOT NULL AND boss_loans_score IS NOT NULL AND boss_loans_score != 11 - stat_roi
rules.description: boss_loans_score must equal exactly 11 - stat_roi when both are non-null. This is a derivation invariant. Maps to pattern ADV-CROSS-COLUMN.
rules.rationale: Spec derivation: 'boss_loans_score = 11 - stat_roi. ROI of 10 -> Loans boss is 1. ROI of 1 -> Loans boss is 10.' Logical model confirms this exact inverse relationship. Any violation is a derivation bug with 0 tolerance.

rules.name: boss_loans null when stat_roi null (null propagation)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE stat_roi IS NULL AND boss_loans_score IS NOT NULL
rules.description: When stat_roi is null, boss_loans_score must also be null. Null propagation invariant from derivation rule.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'Null if stat_roi is null.' boss_loans_score is derived directly from stat_roi. If the input is null, the output must be null. This validates correct null propagation through the derivation chain.

rules.name: stat_ern null when scorecard earnings rank is null (null propagation)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE stat_ern IS NOT NULL AND earnings_1yr_median IS NULL
rules.description: stat_ern should be null when earnings_1yr_median is null, since earnings_1yr_median null indicates the cip_family_earnings_rank input to ERN derivation is also null (both driven by Scorecard privacy suppression).
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'stat_ern bottleneck is cip_family_earnings_rank, null for 64.0% of career_outcomes rows (programs with suppressed earnings).' When earnings are suppressed, the earnings rank that feeds ERN is null, so ERN must be null. P1 because the proxy check (earnings_1yr_median) may not be perfectly correlated with cip_family_earnings_rank in all edge cases.

rules.name: Required NOT NULL fields on program_career_paths
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE record_id IS NULL OR unitid IS NULL OR institution_name IS NULL OR cipcode IS NULL OR program_name IS NULL OR cip_family IS NULL OR cip_family_name IS NULL OR soc_code IS NULL OR match_quality IS NULL OR stats_available_count IS NULL OR bosses_available_count IS NULL OR overall_confidence IS NULL OR promoted_at IS NULL
rules.description: All NOT NULL fields from the logical model must be populated. Covers identity fields, data quality fields, and metadata. Note: occupation_title excluded -- EDA found 22 SOC codes with no title from either BLS or O*NET.
rules.rationale: Logical model defines 12 NOT NULL attributes for program_career_paths. All identity fields (record_id, unitid, institution_name, cipcode, program_name, cip_family, cip_family_name, soc_code) come from the INNER JOIN chain and cannot be null. Data quality fields (match_quality, stats_available_count, bosses_available_count, overall_confidence) are derived with no-null logic. promoted_at is set at promotion. EDA confirms 0% null for all source identity fields. occupation_title is excluded from this P0 rule because EDA found 22 SOC codes with null titles.

rules.name: SOC code format XX-XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE soc_code !~ '^\d{2}-\d{4}$'
rules.description: Every soc_code must match SOC standard format XX-XXXX. This is the join key to BLS and O*NET sources.
rules.rationale: Upstream DQ rules (GLD-OP-004) validate SOC format at source. Crosswalk Silver also validates format. 832 BLS SOC codes and 798 O*NET SOC codes all conform. Any format violation indicates a join chain corruption.

rules.name: CIP code format XX.XX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE cipcode !~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}$'
rules.description: Every cipcode must match the 4-digit Scorecard format XX.XX. This field is carried from career_outcomes, not the crosswalk's 6-digit format.
rules.rationale: Spec: 'cipcode is 4-digit XX.XX format from Scorecard.' Domain context: 'CIPCODE must always be treated as string type (XX.XXXX format), never float.' career_outcomes stores CIP as XX.XX. The program_career_paths table carries the Scorecard CIP, not the crosswalk CIP. Any other format indicates a join key confusion.

rules.name: match_quality consistent with stat nulls (full = stats present)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE match_quality = 'scorecard_only' AND (stat_grw IS NOT NULL OR stat_hmn IS NOT NULL OR boss_market_score IS NOT NULL OR boss_burnout_score IS NOT NULL OR boss_ceiling_score IS NOT NULL)
rules.description: When match_quality is 'scorecard_only' (no BLS or O*NET data), all occupation-sourced stats and bosses must be null. Maps to pattern ADV-CROSS-COLUMN.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'scorecard_only: 830 rows (0.1%) -- no occupation data at all.' The match_quality derivation is based on join success. If both BLS and O*NET joins failed, no occupation-level fields should be populated. P1 because the 24 partial O*NET profiles (with null scores despite row existing) may create edge cases with 'full' match_quality but null HMN/burnout.

rules.name: Referential integrity: soc_code in crosswalk
rules.sql: SELECT DISTINCT p.soc_code FROM consumable.program_career_paths p WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk xw WHERE xw.soc_code = p.soc_code)
rules.description: Every soc_code in program_career_paths must exist in the cip_soc_crosswalk. The crosswalk is the source of SOC codes via the join chain. Maps to pattern ADV-FK-VALID.
rules.rationale: The join chain produces SOC codes exclusively from the crosswalk. Any SOC code not in the crosswalk indicates a join chain corruption or data fabrication. EDA: 867 distinct crosswalk SOC codes, all rows flow through the crosswalk INNER JOIN.

rules.name: Referential integrity: unitid in career_outcomes
rules.sql: SELECT DISTINCT p.unitid FROM consumable.program_career_paths p WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM consumable.career_outcomes co WHERE co.unitid = p.unitid)
rules.description: Every unitid in program_career_paths must exist in career_outcomes. All institutions flow from the Scorecard source. Maps to pattern ADV-FK-VALID.
rules.rationale: career_outcomes is the base of the join chain. All unitid values originate there. Any unitid not in career_outcomes indicates data fabrication during transformation.

rules.name: Grain uniqueness: soc_code x related_soc_code (career_branches)
rules.sql: SELECT soc_code, related_soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.career_branches GROUP BY soc_code, related_soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per soc_code x related_soc_code. This is a 1:1 enrichment of career_transitions. Maps to patterns CONS-GRAIN-UNIQUE and ADV-GRAIN-UNIQUE.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Row count: 15,944 (1:1 with career_transitions).' Logical model: 'Zero duplicates on (soc_code, related_soc_code). 1:1 enrichment of career_transitions.' career_transitions has unique grain (verified by upstream DQ). Threshold: 100% unique.

rules.name: Record ID uniqueness and not null (career_branches)
rules.sql: SELECT record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.career_branches GROUP BY record_id HAVING cnt > 1 UNION ALL SELECT 'NULL_CHECK' AS record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE record_id IS NULL HAVING cnt > 0
rules.description: Surrogate key record_id (computed via compute_grain_id with prefix 'br') must be non-null and unique.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'record_id is PK, NOT NULL.' Deterministic SHA-256 hash of (soc_code, related_soc_code) should produce 0 collisions for 15,944 rows.

rules.name: Row count exactly 15,944 (career_branches)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 15944 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_branches
rules.description: career_branches must contain exactly 15,944 rows -- one per career_transitions row. No rows added or dropped during enrichment.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Row count: 15,944 (1:1 with career_transitions).' Spec: '15,944 (same as career_transitions -- 1:1 enrichment).' Logical model: 'Expected cardinality: 15,944 rows.' This is a 1:1 carry-forward with LEFT JOINs (no row loss).

rules.name: career_branches SOC code format (both columns)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE soc_code !~ '^\d{2}-\d{4}$' OR related_soc_code !~ '^\d{2}-\d{4}$'
rules.description: Both soc_code and related_soc_code must match SOC format XX-XXXX. Carried from career_transitions.
rules.rationale: Upstream career_transitions DQ validates SOC format. All 798 source SOCs and 796 target SOCs conform. Any format violation indicates a carry-forward bug.

rules.name: career_branches stat score ranges 1-10 when non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE (source_grw IS NOT NULL AND (source_grw < 1 OR source_grw > 10)) OR (source_hmn IS NOT NULL AND (source_hmn < 1 OR source_hmn > 10)) OR (source_burnout IS NOT NULL AND (source_burnout < 1 OR source_burnout > 10)) OR (related_grw IS NOT NULL AND (related_grw < 1 OR related_grw > 10)) OR (related_hmn IS NOT NULL AND (related_hmn < 1 OR related_hmn > 10)) OR (related_burnout IS NOT NULL AND (related_burnout < 1 OR related_burnout > 10))
rules.description: All score fields (source_grw, source_hmn, source_burnout, related_grw, related_hmn, related_burnout) must be in 1-10 when non-null. Maps to pattern CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE.
rules.rationale: All scores are carried from upstream Gold tables (occupation_profiles and onet_work_profiles) which enforce 1-10 ranges. EDA report confirms score ranges at source. Any out-of-range value is a carry-forward bug.

rules.name: career_branches stat deltas range -9 to +9
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE (grw_delta IS NOT NULL AND (grw_delta < -9 OR grw_delta > 9)) OR (hmn_delta IS NOT NULL AND (hmn_delta < -9 OR hmn_delta > 9)) OR (burnout_delta IS NOT NULL AND (burnout_delta < -9 OR burnout_delta > 9))
rules.description: Stat deltas (grw_delta, hmn_delta, burnout_delta) must be in -9 to +9 range when non-null. Since both source and target scores are 1-10, the maximum possible delta is 10-1=9 and minimum is 1-10=-9. Maps to pattern CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE.
rules.rationale: Derivation rule: delta = related_score - source_score. With both scores constrained to [1,10], the delta is mathematically constrained to [-9,+9]. Any value outside this range indicates a derivation or input corruption bug.

rules.name: career_branches delta null when source or target null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE (grw_delta IS NOT NULL AND (source_grw IS NULL OR related_grw IS NULL)) OR (hmn_delta IS NOT NULL AND (source_hmn IS NULL OR related_hmn IS NULL)) OR (burnout_delta IS NOT NULL AND (source_burnout IS NULL OR related_burnout IS NULL)) OR (wage_delta IS NOT NULL AND (source_wage IS NULL OR related_wage IS NULL))
rules.description: Delta fields must be null when either the source or target stat is null. Null propagation invariant. Maps to pattern ADV-CROSS-COLUMN.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'Null if either side is null' for all four delta fields. Computing a delta from a null input would be a derivation bug. This validates correct null propagation.

rules.name: career_branches branch_has_full_data consistency
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE branch_has_full_data = TRUE AND (related_grw IS NULL OR related_hmn IS NULL) UNION ALL SELECT * FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE branch_has_full_data = FALSE AND related_grw IS NOT NULL AND related_hmn IS NOT NULL AND related_burnout IS NOT NULL AND related_wage IS NOT NULL
rules.description: branch_has_full_data=True requires related occupation to have both BLS (related_grw) and O*NET (related_hmn) data. branch_has_full_data=False should not have all four related stats populated. Maps to pattern ADV-CROSS-COLUMN.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'True if the related occupation has both BLS occupation_profiles data AND O*NET onet_work_profiles data.' EDA report: 'branch_has_full_data True: 15,390 (96.5%).' The 24 O*NET partial profiles (null HMN/burnout) mean branch_has_full_data may be True while related_hmn is null -- this is an edge case from the semantic gap noted in EDA. P1 to account for this nuance.

rules.name: career_branches branch_has_full_data >= 95%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN branch_has_full_data = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(*) >= 0.95 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_branches
rules.description: At least 95% of career_branches rows should have full data for the related occupation.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'branch_has_full_data True: 15,390 (96.5%).' 96.9% of career_transitions SOCs are found in BLS, 100% in O*NET. Threshold set at 95% with margin.

rules.name: career_branches required NOT NULL fields
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE record_id IS NULL OR soc_code IS NULL OR source_title IS NULL OR related_soc_code IS NULL OR related_title IS NULL OR best_index IS NULL OR relatedness_tier IS NULL OR is_primary IS NULL OR branch_has_full_data IS NULL OR promoted_at IS NULL
rules.description: All NOT NULL fields from the logical model must be populated. Covers identity, classification, derived flag, and metadata.
rules.rationale: Logical model defines 9 NOT NULL attributes for career_branches. All identity fields flow from career_transitions (which has 0% null on these fields). branch_has_full_data and promoted_at are derived/generated with no-null logic. EDA confirms 0% null on all source identity fields.

rules.name: career_branches relatedness_tier valid enum
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE relatedness_tier NOT IN ('Primary-Short', 'Primary-Long', 'Supplemental')
rules.description: relatedness_tier must be one of three defined values. Carried from career_transitions.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Relatedness tier distribution: Primary-Short (4,134), Primary-Long (3,938), Supplemental (7,872).' These are the only three values in the upstream source. Any other value is a carry-forward bug.

rules.name: career_branches referential integrity to career_transitions
rules.sql: SELECT DISTINCT b.soc_code, b.related_soc_code FROM consumable.career_branches b WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM consumable.career_transitions ct WHERE ct.bls_soc_code = b.soc_code AND ct.related_bls_soc_code = b.related_soc_code)
rules.description: Every (soc_code, related_soc_code) pair in career_branches must exist in career_transitions. career_branches is a 1:1 enrichment of career_transitions. Maps to pattern ADV-FK-VALID.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Row count: 15,944 (1:1 with career_transitions).' The join chain starts from career_transitions as the base table. Any pair not in career_transitions indicates data fabrication.

rules.name: Golden dataset verification
rules.sql: WITH chain1 AS (SELECT CASE WHEN stat_ern = 5 AND stat_roi = 8 AND stat_grw = 6 AND stat_hmn = 5 AND boss_loans_score = 3 AND boss_market_score = 8 AND boss_burnout_score = 6 AND boss_ceiling_score = 3 AND match_quality = 'full' AND stats_available_count = 4 AND bosses_available_count = 4 AND overall_confidence = 'high' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE unitid = 151324 AND cipcode = '52.02' AND soc_code = '11-1021'), chain2 AS (SELECT CASE WHEN stat_ern = 4 AND stat_roi = 1 AND stat_grw = 5 AND stat_hmn = 3 AND boss_loans_score = 10 AND boss_market_score = 4 AND boss_burnout_score = 5 AND boss_ceiling_score = 3 AND match_quality = 'full' AND overall_confidence = 'high' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.program_career_paths WHERE unitid = 190725 AND cipcode = '30.18' AND soc_code = '19-2099'), chain3 AS (SELECT CASE WHEN source_grw = 6 AND source_hmn = 5 AND source_burnout = 6 AND related_grw = 8 AND related_hmn = 4 AND related_burnout = 7 AND grw_delta = 2 AND hmn_delta = -1 AND burnout_delta = 1 AND wage_delta = -44720.0 AND branch_has_full_data = true THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE soc_code = '11-1011' AND related_soc_code = '11-3031') SELECT SUM(violation) AS total_violations FROM (SELECT violation FROM chain1 UNION ALL SELECT violation FROM chain2 UNION ALL SELECT violation FROM chain3)
rules.description: Verifies 3 golden dataset chains against pipeline output: (1) ISU Business Admin 52.02 -> SOC 11-1021, (2) Daemen Natural Sciences 30.18 -> SOC 19-2099, (3) Career branch Chief Executives 11-1011 -> Financial Managers 11-3031. All stat/boss scores and derived fields must match expected values from governance/golden-datasets/gold-futureproof-engine-golden.json. Maps to pattern CONS-GOLDEN-DATASET.
rules.rationale: Pattern CONS-GOLDEN-DATASET requires verification against known-correct values. Three chains verified against warehouse on 2026-04-09: Chain 1 traces ISU Business Admin (unitid=151324, cipcode=52.02, soc=11-1021) with stat_ern=5, stat_roi=8, stat_grw=6, stat_hmn=5. Chain 2 traces Daemen Natural Sciences (unitid=190725, cipcode=30.18, soc=19-2099) with stat_roi=1, boss_loans=10 (devastating DTE=4.67). Chain 3 traces career branch 11-1011->11-3031 with grw_delta=2, hmn_delta=-1, burnout_delta=1.

rules.name: wage_delta reasonable range
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_branches WHERE wage_delta IS NOT NULL AND (wage_delta < -250000 OR wage_delta > 250000)
rules.description: wage_delta (related_wage - source_wage) should be within a reasonable range. BLS median wages range from ~$25K to ~$250K, so maximum plausible delta is ~$225K in either direction.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'median_annual_wage null rate 2.8% in occupation_profiles.' BLS wage data ranges from minimum wage occupations (~$25K) to highly compensated ones (~$250K). A delta exceeding $250K in either direction is implausible. P2 because this is a soft reasonableness check. Maps to pattern ADV-VALUE-RANGE.
